mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 are negative in more than 55% of all observed periods, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a critical threshold, it signals significant economic distress and reduced consumer confidence, which negatively impacts corporate profits and drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with low and stable inflation indicate healthy consumer demand without significant cost pressures, fostering an environment conducive to corporate growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month change < 2.5%
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is negative during periods where both conditions are met, or if the Rank IC is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in industrial production after a period of weakness signals a recovery in manufacturing and broader economic activity, which translates into improved corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth > 0.5% after a 6-month decline
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.10) following such triggers in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near the zero lower bound, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces borrowing costs and encourages investment, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is negative or indistinguishable from zero during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts reflect strong demand in the housing sector and broader economic confidence, which stimulates related industries and contributes to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or if the average return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, often leading to tighter monetary policy, which collectively pressures equity valuations and results in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances when 12-month CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth signifies a healthy and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) during periods of GDPC1 growth above 2.0%.
